subject: Science (CBSE)
chapter: The Human Eye and the Colourful World

activities_projects.title: Tracing Refraction Through Prism (NCERT Activity)
activities_projects.objective: Observe deviation and dispersion of light
steps: Fix white paper on board, trace prism outline
Send narrow white light beam through prism
Trace incident, refracted, emergent rays
Measure angle of deviation and note spectrum
activities_projects.evaluation: Ray tracing accuracy and observations (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Dispersion of White Light Spectrum Formation
steps: Create narrow beam using slit in cardboard
Pass through prism and project on screen
Observe and label VIBGYOR order
Explain wavelength dependence
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Explains dispersion and rainbow formation basics

activities_projects.title: Tyndall Effect and Scattering Demonstration
steps: Dilute milk in water
Shine beam sideways; observe blue from side, reddish from end
Vary concentration and note changes
Link to atmospheric scattering
activities_projects.evaluation: Observations, diagrams, relation to sky/sunset (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Vision Defects Correction Ray Diagrams
activities_projects.objective: Draw and explain correction for myopia/hypermetropia
steps: Draw normal eye, myopic eye (far point)
Show concave lens correction with rays
Repeat for hypermetropia with convex lens
Label focal points and retina image
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Masters ray diagrams for defect correction

activities_projects.title: Atmospheric Refraction Phenomena Chart
steps: Illustrate layered atmosphere density gradient
Draw curved path of starlight/ sunlight
Explain twinkling vs planets, early sunrise/late sunset
Add real photos or sketches

activities_projects.title: Rainbow Formation Model/Diagram
steps: Draw raindrop with incident ray paths
Show refraction, reflection, dispersion inside drop
Label primary/secondary rainbow angles
Explain observer position

activities_projects.title: Power of Accommodation Simulation
materials: Convex lens (adjustable focus if possible), chart
steps: Use lens to focus near/far objects
Relate to ciliary muscles changing lens curvature
Discuss range (near point to infinity)
Draw accommodation diagrams

activities_projects.title: Chapter Concept Quiz and Discussion
steps: Create 20 questions on eye parts, defects, light phenomena
Conduct team quiz with explanations
Discuss common misconceptions

activities_projects.title: Daily Observations of Colour Phenomena Log
steps: Observe over 5 days: sky color, sunset/sunrise, distant objects haze
Note conditions (time, weather)
Explain using scattering/refraction

activities_projects.title: Innovative Device to Show Dispersion/Scattering
steps: Build mini spectroscope or scattering viewer
Test and demonstrate spectrum/scattering
Explain working principle
